data.title: Costco Wholesale March Sales Rise
data.description:
ISSAQUAH (dpa-AFX) - Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) on Thursday reported a rise in net sales for the month of March.

For the five-week period to April 5, the company posted net sales of $28.41 billion, higher than the $25.51 billion in the same period last year.

Net sales for the first 31 weeks were $173.26 billion, up from $158.87 billion last year.

data.title: Austria's OMV records higher energy prices in Q1
data.description:
Austrian oil and gas group OMV recorded higher average energy prices in the first quarter of 2026, it said on Thursday, as the average price of natural gas rose by 17.8% compared to the fourth quarter.

Average realized crude oil price rose to $73.8 per barrel, the Vienna-based group said.

data.title: IXIC: Nasdaq Futures Drop After Strong Rally – Will the Fragile Ceasefire Hold?
data.description:
Key points:
  • Tech stocks swing again
  • US-Iran truce under pressure
  • Best day for Dow in over a year

Hours after the US and Iran announced a two-week truce, Iran accused the US of breaching the ceasefire deal.

📉 Futures Slip After Big Bounce

  • Futures contracts tied to the Nasdaq Composite edged lower about 0.2% after Wednesday’s powerful rally, when the index surged nearly 3% on news of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran. Relief trades rarely move in straight lines.
  • The S&P 500 and Dow futures also softened slightly, suggesting traders are pausing rather than panicking. Markets rallied fast. Now they are checking the fine print.
  • In short, optimism remains alive, but confidence looks conditional. Wednesday was the best day since April 2025 for the Dow Jones after the 30-stock index added 1,300 points.

🕊️ Ceasefire Already Tested

  • Hours after announcing the truce, Iran accused the US of violating the agreement, citing continued regional military activity in Lebanon and disputes over uranium enrichment rights.
  • President Trump warned that any breach would trigger a stronger response than earlier strikes. In other words, another “here we go again” moment for traders trying to make sense of it all.
  • A ceasefire that starts with accusations tends to trade more like a fad than a resolution.

📊 Tech Still Leads the Reaction

  • The Nasdaq tends to react fastest to geopolitical swings because tech valuations depend heavily on stable interest rates and predictable growth expectations. Uncertainty raises the discount rate investors apply to future profits.
  • Wednesday’s surge showed how quickly sentiment can flip when risk fades. Thursday’s pullback shows how fragile that optimism still is.
  • Besides the conflict, investors will be watching for Thursday’s Core PCE print. The Fed’s preferred inflation gauge is expected to show prices rose 3% from a year ago.

data.title: Bouygues Construction wins $1.7 billion contract in US Virgin Islands
data.description:
French conglomerate Bouygues said on Thursday its construction business in the U.S. and the Caribbean had won a contract for a combined value of 1.45 billion euros ($1.67 billion) to rebuild and modernise schools and administrative buildings in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

The projects will take five years, the company said in a statement.

($1 = 0.8574 euros)

data.title: FedEx Pilots Reach Tentative Labor Deal
data.description:
MEMPHIS (dpa-AFX) - Air Line Pilots Association, representing pilots at FedEx Corp. (FDX), a transportation, e-commerce, and business services company, on Wednesday reached a tentative agreement with FedEx on an amended collective bargaining agreement following mediated negotiations.

The agreement was reached during discussions conducted under the supervision of the National Mediation Board.

The tentative agreement will be presented to the FedEx Master Executive Council for review.

If approved for distribution, the agreement will be subject to a ratification vote by FedEx pilots.

Capt. Jose Nieves, chair of the FedEx ALPA Master Executive Council, said the agreement follows lengthy and intensive mediation, with the next step being MEC review and providing pilots with details to evaluate the terms.

On Wednesday, FedEx closed trading 4.60% higher at $373.43 on the New York Stock Exchange. In the after hours, the stock traded 0.17% lesser at $372.80.

data.title: Indonesia to Implement B50 Starting July, but Feedstock Issues Remain — OPIS
data.description:
Underlying feedstock availability issues remain unresolved despite plans for Indonesia to raise its biodiesel blend mandate to 50% starting July 1, a market source said on Wednesday.

Energy Minister Bahlil Lahadalia said the decision follows around six months of successful trials involving B50 used in various vehicles and heavy equipment, according to state media on Monday.

The increase in biodiesel use can support the local palm oil industry while reducing reliance on imported fuel, which has been subject to price surges and supply disruption from the Middle East conflict, he added.

However, the total production volume of crude palm oil--the feedstock for biodiesel--and other palm oil products such as cooking oil is not expected to increase significantly this year, market sources said previously.

Indonesia is likely to reduce CPO exports and ensure that the domestic CPO market is not affected by higher biodiesel production.

In addition, biodiesel producers in the country may face issues sourcing more methanol, another crucial feedstock in biodiesel production.

With the Middle East--especially Iran and Saudi Arabia--being a key methanol supplier to Asia, the regional conflict has disrupted methanol shipments to Southeast Asia. Higher methanol prices will add to higher production costs, which eat into the margins for biodiesel producers, the market source said.

Indonesian biodiesel producers typically sign annual contracts with the government stating that the latter will commit to taking a certain volume of biodiesel from them at a price formula of a fixed-price alpha added to a monthly CPO reference price.

The fixed-price alpha is adjusted annually based on expected production costs, which include the procurement costs of methanol and catalysts, and utilities costs, which do not fluctuate significantly throughout the year.

However, with the Middle East conflict having raised these costs, biodiesel producers may approach the government to adjust the price formula, the source said.

Biodiesel use appears attractive now because high gasoil prices have reduced the price difference between biodiesel and gasoil. However, once the conflict ends and gasoil prices retreat, the higher biodiesel blend will result in higher costs for buyers and users, and the government will need to provide more subsidies to offset the higher biodiesel costs, the source added.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Kite Chong, kchong@opisnet.com; Editing by Mei-Hwen Wong, mwong@opisnet.com

data.title: IMF strikes staff-level deal with Sri Lanka on $700 million in funding
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund said on Thursday it reached a staff-level pact with Sri Lanka following reviews of its reform programme, which stands to unlock financing of about $700 million once approved.

Sri Lanka's economic reforms have supported the recovery, the IMF added, although it has been significantly exposed to the Iran war.

"Sri Lanka is significantly exposed to the Middle East conflict, which has heightened energy prices, disrupted a key air hub for tourists, and affected Sri Lankans working in the region," said Evan Papageorgiou, the IMF's mission chief.

The island nation is recovering from its worst economic crisis in more than seven decades, which led to a foreign debt default in 2022 and a $2.9-billion IMF bailout program.

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on April 9
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Thursday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

March net foreign reserves (ZAFXRS=ECI)

February manufacturing production (ZAMAN=ECI)

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

South Africa's rand, government bonds and stocks surged on Wednesday, paring some of the losses amassed since the start of the Iran war after U.S. President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was last up 6.2%.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian share markets were in a sober mood on Thursday as cracks quickly began to appear in the fragile Gulf truce, nudging oil prices back up and reminding investors the inflationary fallout would last a long time yet.

WALL STREET

U.S. stocks closed sharply higher on Wednesday after a last-minute, two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran lifted investor sentiment.

GOLD

Gold prices held steady on Thursday as market players remained cautious about the direction of U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks, with a key U.S. inflation report due later in the day also in focus for interest rate clues.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

NEWS24

- Fuel surcharge concerns mount as industry points to 'serious under-recovery'

- 13 new Audi models coming to SA as part of ‘product offensive’

data.title: U.S. March CPI Data Likely to Show Impact of Energy Shock — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. CPI data on Friday are expected to represent the first meaningful pass-through of the Iran energy shock, with headline inflation accelerating, driven largely by a surge in energy costs, First Citizens Bank's Phil Neuhart says in a note. "With core inflation more contained, albeit still materially above target, the Federal Reserve will look through the energy-driven spike for now," the head of market and economic research says. This would reinforce a prolonged Fed pause and keep potential rate cuts firmly tied to energy-price normalization, he says. Economists in The Wall Street Journal's poll see headline CPI rising 3.3% in March, versus 2.4% in February, and core inflation at 2.7%, up from 2.5%. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)
